rationale: Implements Solution B from issue #14260 cohesively. Migration `010` (and enterprise `114`) add `acp_session_id` and `acp_session_cwd` columns to `conversation_metadata` with symmetric `downgrade`s. `AppConversationInfo` Pydantic model and `StoredConversationMetadata` SQLAlchemy mapping gain the two fields; `save_app_conversation_info` and `_to_info` persist/load them; new `SQLAppConversationInfoService.update_acp_session` writes both columns plus `last_updated_at`, with a silent skip when the row is not visible to the user context. `live_status_app_conversation_service.py` introduces `_ACP_PERSIST_ROOT='/workspace'`, `_ACP_PERSIST_ENV_BY_SERVER` mapping `claude-code` to `CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR` and `codex` to `CODEX_HOME` (Gemini correctly omitted), and `_acp_persist_env(server)` helper. `_build_acp_start_conversation_request` injects the persist env into `merged_acp_env` so user-supplied `acp_env` entries still override defaults, then guards the resume signal with `'acp_resume_session_id' in type(acp_agent).model_fields` so the PR can land before the paired SDK bump. `webhook_router.on_event` walks `ConversationStateUpdateEvent` instances, filters to `key=='agent_state'` and dict values containing the ACP-specific keys, then calls `update_acp_session(conversation_id, acp_session_id, acp_session_cwd)` wrapped in `try/except Exception` that logs via `_logger.exception` (legitimate isolation so a DB failure does not break the event pipeline, justified inline). Tests cover all three regions: 6 build-request tests (per-provider env, user override precedence, resume id flow, SDK feature-detect skip), 5 SQL persistence tests (column round-trip, overwrite, unknown-conv silent skip, save/get round-trip, default-None), 5 webhook tests (extract, non-ACP ignore, non-state-event ignore, partial cwd-only, DB failure isolation).
